tagline: Where developers are doing their best work.
subheadline: Ship more with local and cloud agents working together.
value_proposition: An AI-powered coding IDE that combines a local agentic assistant (Cascade) with an autonomous cloud agent (Devin) so developers can delegate, parallelize, and ship code faster.
target_audience: Software developers and engineering teams — from individual builders to enterprise organizations — who want AI to handle boilerplate, debugging, and complex tasks while they stay in flow.

testimonials: Every single one of these engineers has to spend literally just one day making projects with Windsurf and it will be like they strapped on rocket boosters. — Garry Tan, President & CEO
Windsurf is so much better than Cursor. It just makes the steps easier. — Avi Schiffmann
I think @windsurf_ai will win the AI Coding Assistant wars. — Andrew Brown
Unreal. I just built an app with 1 prompt. — Alex Finn
I've been exclusively using Windsurf for the past 3 weeks. They are not paying me to say this. It's really good. Really really good. — The Jack Forge
Windsurf makes coding insanely fun and fast! — elvis (@omarsar0)
press: 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant (referenced in footer nav)

pricing.model: self-serve with enterprise tier; token-based usage plans
pricing.price_anchoring: Pricing page exists but no prices shown on landing page; value framed around productivity multiplier ('rocket boosters', '94% code written by AI') rather than cost; blog references 'clear and fair token usage plans'

features: Cascade — agentic local AI with deep codebase understanding, real-time awareness, and collaborative flow
Devin — autonomous cloud agent for delegating debugging, testing, and deployment while coding locally
Windsurf Tab — multi-action autocomplete exclusive to the Windsurf Editor (cursor movement, imports, full completions)
MCP Support — one-click connection to external tools and services (Figma, Slack, Stripe, Playwright, etc.)
Spaces — bundles agent sessions, PRs, files, and shared context around a single task
Agent Command Center — unified Kanban dashboard managing all local Cascade and cloud Devin sessions
differentiators: 'The most powerful way to code with AI' (Cascade claim)
Devin is built directly into the IDE — no separate tool or context switch
'Full power of Windsurf Tab is exclusive to the Windsurf Editor' — IDE plugins get autocomplete only
Local + cloud agents working in parallel — 'close your laptop and come back later'
94% of code written by AI — highest productivity claim in the category
JetBrains native plugin — not just VS Code
trust_signals: Garry Tan (Y Combinator President & CEO) named endorsement
Windsurf for Government offering (implies compliance/security posture)
Named enterprise customer logos (Reply shown in marquee)

design_patterns.layout: Long-form vertical scroll with full-width hero, alternating feature sections with UI mockups, social proof marquee, testimonial carousel, stats band, and download CTA footer
design_patterns.color_scheme: Dark/near-black background with bright accent colors; high-contrast UI screenshots
design_patterns.typography: Modern sans-serif; large bold headlines, smaller descriptive body text
design_patterns.visual_style: Technical and bold — product-screenshot-forward with a dark IDE aesthetic
notable_patterns: Auto-scrolling logo marquee for customer logos
Side-by-side feature sections with animated product UI mockups
Horizontal testimonial carousel with Twitter-style cards
Stats band with large number callouts
Kanban-style UI mockup for Agent Command Center feature

emotional_tone: Aspirational and confident — positions coding with Windsurf as a qualitative leap ('rocket boosters', 'hysterical laughter', 'insanely fun'), not just an incremental improvement
key_objections_addressed: Context-switching fatigue — Spaces and Agent Command Center keep everything in one place
AI breaking your flow — Cascade is framed as keeping you in flow state, not interrupting it
AI tools being too hands-on — Devin lets you fully delegate and walk away
Enterprise trust — dedicated Enterprise page, Gartner recognition, Government offering, Security page
Vendor lock-in / model dependency — 'first-class support for every major model provider'
Cursor comparison — multiple testimonials directly name Windsurf as better than Cursor
missing_or_weak: No pricing visible on the landing page — forces an extra click before conversion decision
Logo wall is very thin — only 'Reply' visible in the marquee despite 4,000+ enterprise claim
No free trial or freemium CTA — only 'Download' with no mention of what's free vs paid
No video or live demo embedded — feature mockups are static or light animation
Testimonials are all Twitter posts with no company/title attribution beyond Garry Tan
No comparison table vs Cursor/Copilot despite multiple testimonials referencing them
Devin is framed as 'new' but no clear pricing or availability context for it
No explicit security or compliance callout in the hero/main flow — buried in footer
standout_elements: The 94% AI-written code stat is an unusually bold and specific credibility claim
Garry Tan quote is a high-signal named endorsement from a recognizable tech figure
Cursor is beaten without being named — social proof does the competitive work organically
Devin-in-IDE positioning is a genuine product differentiator clearly explained with a use-case narrative
Testimonial carousel with real Twitter handles adds authenticity over generic pull quotes
MCP support with one-click setup is called out as a UX advantage over competitors
Windsurf for Government in footer signals serious enterprise/compliance maturity
